description: Techniques for ruffing losers, managing side suits for discards, and advanced plays like dummy reversal and loser-on-loser.

hands.title: 118. Ruffing losers in dummy
hands.notes: Ruff diamond losers in dummy using dummy's small trumps. Each ruff in the short hand gains an extra trick.

hands.title: 119. Ruffing losers in hand
hands.notes: Ruff a club loser in the South hand. While ruffing in the long hand does not usually gain, here it is needed to avoid losing too many club tricks.

hands.title: 120. Setting up a ruff
hands.notes: Play Ace and another diamond to set up a ruff in dummy. You must lose one diamond first to be able to ruff the next one.

hands.title: 121. Giving yourself a ruffing entry
hands.notes: Use a diamond ruff in dummy as an entry to reach dummy's winners. The ruff serves double duty: eliminating a loser and providing entry.

hands.title: 122. Ruffing before drawing trumps
hands.notes: Ruff a diamond in dummy before drawing trumps. If you draw trumps first, dummy has no trumps left for the ruff.

hands.title: 123. High ruff vs low ruff
hands.notes: Ruff with the Ten of hearts in dummy rather than a small trump, to prevent an overruff. The high ruff costs nothing since the Ten would not win a trick otherwise.

hands.title: 124. Overruffing
hands.notes: When a defender ruffs with a small trump, overruff with a higher trump to win the trick. Basic but essential technique.

hands.title: 125. Avoiding being overruffed
hands.notes: Ruff high enough that no defender can overruff. Consider what trumps the opponents hold before choosing which trump to ruff with.

hands.title: 126. Ruff-sluff basics
hands.notes: If a defender leads a suit where both declarer and dummy are void, declarer gets a ruff-sluff: ruff in one hand and discard a loser from the other.

hands.title: 127. Ruffing to establish a suit
hands.notes: Ruff diamonds in dummy to establish the long diamonds. After two ruffs, the remaining diamonds are winners for discards.

hands.title: 128. Ruffing the third round of a suit
hands.notes: Play Ace and a diamond, losing one. When you regain the lead, ruff the third diamond in dummy. A common and effective technique.

hands.title: 129. Dummy reversal basics
hands.notes: Ruff diamonds in the long hand (South) repeatedly, then draw trumps using dummy's trumps. This reverses the normal roles: dummy becomes the master hand.

hands.title: 130. Ruffing to shorten trumps intentionally
hands.notes: Shorten your trump holding intentionally by ruffing, reducing to the same length as a defender's trumps. This is preparation for a trump coup or endplay.

hands.title: 131. Avoiding premature ruffs
hands.notes: Do not ruff a trick that you do not need to ruff. Discarding a loser may be better than ruffing, especially when it shortens your trump holding dangerously.

hands.title: 132. Using a side suit as a source of discards
hands.notes: Establish dummy's club suit as a source of discards. After drawing trumps, play clubs to discard diamond losers from the South hand.

hands.title: 133. Throwing losers on winners
hands.notes: Cash dummy's club winners to throw diamond losers from the South hand. This is the simplest discard technique: pitch losers on established winners.

hands.title: 134. Loser-on-loser play
hands.notes: Instead of ruffing a losing trick, discard another loser from the opposite hand. This loser-on-loser play avoids an overruff and loses only one trick instead of risking two.

hands.title: 135. Discarding from the right hand
hands.notes: Choose which hand to discard from carefully. Discard from the hand that has losers in suits you cannot protect, keeping guards in critical suits.

hands.title: 136. Discard timing
hands.notes: Time your discards correctly. Take discards on established winners before the defense can ruff them. Delay draws if needed to cash discard sources first.
